title: RenodeServer<->Simulation time-model mismatch (BARRIER_SYNC stalls at t=0; duplicate RX delivery)
description: The RenodeServer TCP bridge (python/src/lichen/sim/renode_server.py) uses a non-blocking RX_POLL and never puts a node into RX_WAIT. With the Simulation default TimeMode.BARRIER_SYNC, maybe_advance_time() only advances when a node is in RX_WAIT, so sim time stays at 0: transmissions never expire and get_rx_result() re-delivers the same packet on every poll (observed ~80+ duplicate rx_success for a single tx_id, though metrics dedup). In TimeMode.REALTIME time advances correctly, but the non-blocking bridge polls do not reliably fall inside a transmission's airtime window, so identical/lockstep nodes miss each other. Decide the correct time model for the Renode bridge (likely REALTIME with the RX poll integrated into airtime windows, or a bridge-driven RX_WAIT in barrier mode) and make 2-node RX deterministic. Relates to project-LICHEN-r7h4.6.
acceptance_criteria: Documented time-model choice for the Renode bridge; a 2-node Renode run delivers each frame exactly once to the receiving firmware.
